title: Luke Kanies | Building on ATProto
content_html:
Building on ATProto

Posted 7/20/26

Bluesky’s Atmosphere Protocol has the chance to become the base for a whole generation of applications. But it’s developing in surprising and disappointing ways.

I spent part of last week at the Local First Conference in Berlin. I was not surprised to find that AI coding was on everyone’s minds. Less expected was how pervasive ATProto was.

I’ve been considering trying to build on ATProto for a few months, so this was a great chance to ask a bunch of questions from both people building on it, and the people actually building it (multiple key Bluesky people were there).

I want ATProto to be the answer for me. I want to again live in a world where application developers use public, community driven standards to create interoperable applications with common data standards. I think it can be. But unfortunately I think it’s not on track to be that right now.

In this post I’m going to describe what I have been wanting to build, and how ATProto’s current and proposed designs do and don’t work with that. And I’ll also throw in some comments about what’s been surprising to me.

I’m probably trying to do too much here. But my main goal is to provide feedback on the proposed design, and it’s hard to do so without being clear about what I want to build and why.

Start With Reviews

The smallest version of what I want to build is a suite of applications for recording reviews. In an ideal world, these applications would supplant Yelp, GoodReads, Letterboxd, and a bunch of similar applications for just about anything you might consider reviewing. (Yes, I know there are already people building versions of these on ATProto; I’d want to collaborate with them.)

I want to replace these applications not because of their function, but because of their business models. Neither my wife nor I will use them the way they’re intended, and I think most people are like one or the other of us.

Local First Data

For me, I don’t want these companies to own my data. We have more than a thousand bookmarks in Yelp, but… they’re stuck in Yelp. I can’t do anything else useful with them: I can’t publish them, I can’t share them, I can’t write scripts across them, I can’t version control them. They belong to Yelp, not me. And, frankly, the experience they provide for managing lists of bookmarks kinda sucks.

This is a classic local first use case. I could adopt one tool that was great for writing reviews, another that used the same data but was better at publishing best-of lists for my friends, and another that made it easy to share “best business books” lists on my web site. No single app is going to do all of these, but a shared, public data model allows everyone to have what they want.

I’ve lost the willingness to give big corporations a permanent right to control how I use my own data. (That’s why I’m writing this in plain text and publishing via Hugo.) The apps only get worse over time. And if I’m going to invest that much time in recording my opinions, I want to control its use, not let Yelp do it.

Public and Private

As to my wife, she will just never publish anything online. Never. If I want her to review books or restaurants or recipes, it has to be in a place where I – and maybe the rest of our family – will see it, but no one else.

Personally, I am willing to publish public reviews. And sometimes I specifically want to, like sharing books I found useful as a founder. But doing so is complicated. The information I would record about a restaurant for myself is quite different from what I would want the wider world to know. Yet… most apps don’t allow you to draw this distinction. Reviews are public. Your opinion affects the subject’s reputation, even if you didn’t want it to. (I’m vegetarian and autistic, so a place working for me is not necessarily relevant for other people.)

Fundamentally, most of these applications are built to enable influencers: People who want to build a following online for their reviews. “Oh man, she has the best book recommendations!” “If he likes the restaurant, I know I will too!”

But most of us don’t want to become influencers. If anything, we’ve generally learned the lesson that it sucks to be followed by a bunch of people online. I want to keep my stuff private. I don’t want my opinions to matter to anyone else.

I mean sure, when someone comes to town I want to be able to give them a list of restaurants they should visit and activities they should do. But I want to send it to them, not the rest of the world.

We can split the world into three kinds of people:

  • Those who would only record a review if other people will read it. These are the influencer-wannabes
  • People like me, who would provide a mix of public and private reviews, depending on the circumstance
  • People like my wife who will only ever record reviews if they can be confident it will be private to just their friend group, or even just to themselves

The current crop of applications works fine for the first group. But it leaves the latter two out in the cold.

And I bet anything that those other two groups are much larger.

So, what we need is a system that allows application developers to easily let their users choose how public or private they want to be – entirely public, entirely private, or sharing piecemeal with individual groups.

What does this have to do with ATProto?

Let’s start with what’s great about ATProto: It seems to be the first protocol designed to solve identity at scale, enabling any application to build ATProto identities into their application for authentication, tracking followers and following, and the other related features that basically every application needs.

Its identity system not perfect (I wish it were more human readable, especially). But it seems to be close enough today that nearly every speaker at Local First this year mentioned it. No more does every app developer have to create their own social graph, their own identity and authentication systems, their own means of finding friends.

Unfortunately, at least for now, the rest of the protocol is of limited use for my goals.

Today, ATProto is public-only: It assumes that everything you do will be published online for the whole world to see. That design decision is baked into everything from the storage systems to the structure of the publishing services.

The community is currently designing what they call “permissioned data”. (I think this is a silly name, and it should just be called “private data”, which is better both because “private” is actually a word, and also because it describes user behavior instead of technical implementation, which is a far better naming practice).

Unfortunately, while most of the assumptions driving the design seem reasonable, the resulting design looks very hard to build on. A lot of where I think it goes wrong is captured perfectly in the above linked post:

Before we get into it, the through-line on all of this is that public broadcast data is substantially different from permissioned data.

I fundamentally disagree with this. (I am not the only one.)

Private and public data are basically identical. A restaurant review is a restaurant review whether I am the only one who ever sees it or it gets shouted to the rooftops. A book review I share with my book club is identical to a book review I share with my wife or publish on my web site.

Data I publish to the world is just a special case: The permission is world-read. Just like data I never publish is a special case: It has no readers other than me.

Unfortunately ATProto is already used out in the wild for Bluesky, and it is designed and built as a public-only protocol. It would be hard to change it to support access control, limited distribution, and the other features you need with private data.

So, instead, the community has, from what I can tell, just… designed a completely independent system for private data.

Permissioned Data relies on the existing identity system and lexicons (for defining data types). But adds entirely new data structures, plus new methods for managing and validating that data. (Note that the link above is to one in a series of posts on the design; you can get to all of them from there.)

So, as an app developer, you have to essentially write two applications: One for public data, and one for private. But, your users don’t think of it as two apps. “These are restaurant reviews. All the restaurant reviews should go together.” So your job, as the developer, is to support these two data systems, and two protocols, but never let the user see that they’re totally different.

That gets messy real fast. You’ve got a private post you want to make public? You’re not modifying it – you’re deleting the old one and making a new one in the public subsystem. Does it keep its likes? Its reposts? Links to it? 🤷‍♂️ No idea, but… probably not.

It’s actually worse than that. ATProto’s storage subsystem, the “Personal Data Server”, provides direct access to your data. In other words, you should expect more than one application to want to read and write your data. So now, anyone who is interested in this data has to write two versions, but lie to their users and hide any differences.

Because, again, there aren’t actual differences. The actual data being stored is the same. The way the user thinks about it is the same.

To me this is a sign that the current proposal is flawed.

I don’t know how to fix it. I’m nowhere near close enough to conversation to think I can propose solutions yet. But hopefully I can help people see problems, at least.

One last local-first note

I only had a high-level understanding of ATProto went I went to Local First, so I learned a lot while I was there. One thing I was definitely wrong about was how the PDS worked.

I naively assumed it worked a lot like a git repository. The ATProto community regularly says that I own my own data. I tend to assume that means that I have a copy of it, and that when I make changes, I am operating against my copy then distributing it. This is exactly how git works: I clone a repository, make my changes, then push them back up to the server for distribution. I have a hard time imagining “owning” my data without always having a copy of it.

But no, that’s not at all how ATProto works. The PDS is “your” server, but… it’s actually a server. You do not push data up and pull it down; you speak a protocol to it. You only “own” your data because it and the protocols for accessing it are public, and you can change where it is stored.

It does use cryptography-based data integrity guarantees like git does, which is one more reason I assumed I could easily manage the data like git does. But those guarantees are only used on the server, not in the protocols you use to provide new data.

ATProto meets about half of the design principles in the Local First essay linked above. But it clearly misses on the data being at your fingertips or the network being optional, and also misses out on a lot of implied abilities because you’re always talking to a remote server.

If you want to record a review while you’re offline… best practice is to essentially store those reviews in a temporary area, then push them to the server when you’re back online. In other words, you have to build your own custom storage and sync system if you want offline support. Obviously not impossible, but also obviously not local-first.

If you then cross this with the current design for Permissioned Data, I’ve got a bit of a mess:

  • Custom local storage for offline data
  • Custom sync system for emptying offline data
  • Sync system must use different protocols for public and private data
  • Online app usage must also use separate read and write systems for private and public data, and is likely different from the system used for offline support

As an application developer committed to both local first principles and letting the user choose between public and private, ATProto is working at least as much against me as with me. Am I really better off building on this, versus designing my own system? That question is doubly scary when you recognize that the protocol designer and I are so far apart philosophically.

If I believe public and private data are fundamentally the same, just with different access rights, but the community believes they are and should be different… I am fighting the protocol, storage system, and community every step of the way. I’ve done this before, and it sucks.

Conclusion

I’m still excited about ATProto. And thankfully, the Permissioned Data design is early enough that it can still be improved. And I’m not the only person out there pushing back on the existing design and its goals.

Even if the proposed design goes through, I can build at least somewhat on ATProto, maybe using its identity and lexicon systems.

But I came up in the 90s, when the entire internet was built on standardized protocols that were internet-scale, resilient, maintained by the community and a standards group, and built to empower its users rather than enrich whoever could build the biggest moat. I want that world again.

I think ATProto has the opportunity to be the first new protocol of this type in decades. I hope they go for it, and build something every application developer (but especially me!) wants to build on.

I can’t do anything else useful with them: I can’t publish them, I can’t share them, I can’t write scripts across them, I can’t version control them. They belong to Yelp, not me. And, frankly, the experience they provide for managing lists of bookmarks kinda sucks.

sentences.en: For me, I don’t want these companies to own my data.
sentences.zh: 对我来说，我不希望这些公司拥有我的数据。

sentences.en: We have more than a thousand bookmarks in Yelp, but…
sentences.zh: 我们在 Yelp 中有一千多个书签，但是……

sentences.en: they’re stuck in Yelp.
sentences.zh: 他们被困在 Yelp 中。

sentences.en: I can’t do anything else useful with them: I can’t publish them, I can’t share them, I can’t write scripts across them, I can’t version control them.
sentences.zh: 我不能用它们做任何其他有用的事情：我不能发布它们，我不能共享它们，我不能在它们上编写脚本，我不能对它们进行版本控制。

sentences.en: They belong to Yelp, not me.
sentences.zh: 它们属于 Yelp，而不是我。

sentences.en: And, frankly, the experience they provide for managing lists of bookmarks kinda sucks.
sentences.zh: 而且，坦率地说，他们提供的管理书签列表的体验有点糟糕。

blocks.html_en: This is a classic local first use case. I could adopt one tool that was great for writing reviews, another that used the same data but was better at publishing best-of lists for my friends, and another that made it easy to share “best business books” lists on my web site. No single app is going to do all of these, but a shared, public data model allows everyone to have what they want.

sentences.en: This is a classic local first use case.
sentences.zh: 这是一个经典的本地优先用例。

sentences.en: I could adopt one tool that was great for writing reviews, another that used the same data but was better at publishing best-of lists for my friends, and another that made it easy to share “best business books” lists on my web site.
sentences.zh: 我可以采用一种非常适合撰写评论的工具，另一种使用相同数据但更擅长为我的朋友发布最佳书籍列表的工具，以及另一种可以轻松在我的网站上共享“最佳商业书籍”列表的工具。

sentences.en: No single app is going to do all of these, but a shared, public data model allows everyone to have what they want.
sentences.zh: 没有任何一个应用程序能够完成所有这些工作，但是共享的公共数据模型可以让每个人都拥有他们想要的东西。

blocks.html_en: I’ve lost the willingness to give big corporations a permanent right to control how I use my own data. (That’s why I’m writing this in plain text and publishing via Hugo.) The apps only get worse over time. And if I’m going to invest that much time in recording my opinions, I want to control its use, not let Yelp do it.

sentences.en: I’ve lost the willingness to give big corporations a permanent right to control how I use my own data.
sentences.zh: 我已经不再愿意给予大公司永久的权利来控制我如何使用自己的数据。

sentences.en: (That’s why I’m writing this in plain text and publishing via Hugo.)
sentences.zh: （这就是为什么我以纯文本形式写这篇文章并通过 Hugo 发布。）

sentences.en: The apps only get worse over time.
sentences.zh: 随着时间的推移，这些应用程序只会变得更糟。

sentences.en: And if I’m going to invest that much time in recording my opinions, I want to control its use, not let Yelp do it.
sentences.zh: 如果我要投入那么多时间来记录我的意见，我想控制它的使用，而不是让 Yelp 这么做。

blocks.html_en: Public and Private

sentences.en: Public and Private
sentences.zh: 公共和私人

blocks.html_en: As to my wife, she will just never publish anything online. Never. If I want her to review books or restaurants or recipes, it has to be in a place where I – and maybe the rest of our family – will see it, but no one else.

sentences.en: As to my wife, she will just never publish anything online.
sentences.zh: 至于我的妻子，她永远不会在网上发布任何内容。

sentences.en: Never.
sentences.zh: 绝不。

sentences.en: If I want her to review books or restaurants or recipes, it has to be in a place where I – and maybe the rest of our family – will see it, but no one else.
sentences.zh: 如果我想让她评论书籍、餐馆或食谱，它必须放在一个我——也许还有我们家里的其他人——能看到但其他人看不到的地方。

blocks.html_en: Personally, I am willing to publish public reviews. And sometimes I specifically want to, like sharing books I found useful as a founder. But doing so is complicated. The information I would record about a restaurant for myself is quite different from what I would want the wider world to know. Yet… most apps don’t allow you to draw this distinction. Reviews are public. Your opinion affects the subject’s reputation, even if you didn’t want it to. (I’m vegetarian and autistic, so a place working for me is not necessarily relevant for other people.)

sentences.en: Personally, I am willing to publish public reviews.
sentences.zh: 就我个人而言，我愿意发表公众评论。

sentences.en: And sometimes I specifically want to, like sharing books I found useful as a founder.
sentences.zh: 有时我特别想分享我认为作为创始人有用的书籍。

sentences.en: But doing so is complicated.
sentences.zh: 但这样做很复杂。

sentences.en: The information I would record about a restaurant for myself is quite different from what I would want the wider world to know.
sentences.zh: 我为自己记录的有关餐厅的信息与我希望更广泛的世界了解的信息截然不同。

sentences.en: Yet…
sentences.zh: 然而…

sentences.en: most apps don’t allow you to draw this distinction.
sentences.zh: 大多数应用程序不允许您进行这种区分。

sentences.en: Reviews are public.
sentences.zh: 评论是公开的。

sentences.en: Your opinion affects the subject’s reputation, even if you didn’t want it to.
sentences.zh: 你的观点会影响对象的声誉，即使你不希望如此。

sentences.en: (I’m vegetarian and autistic, so a place working for me is not necessarily relevant for other people.)
sentences.zh: （我是素食主义者并且患有自闭症，所以为我工作的地方不一定与其他人相关。）

blocks.html_en: Fundamentally, most of these applications are built to enable influencers: People who want to build a following online for their reviews. “Oh man, she has the best book recommendations!” “If he likes the restaurant, I know I will too!”

sentences.en: Fundamentally, most of these applications are built to enable influencers: People who want to build a following online for their reviews.
sentences.zh: 从根本上来说，这些应用程序中的大多数都是为了支持影响者而构建的：那些想要在网上建立追随者的评论的人。

sentences.en: “Oh man, she has the best book recommendations!”
sentences.zh: “天哪，她推荐了最好的书！”

sentences.en: “If he likes the restaurant, I know I will too!”
sentences.zh: “如果他喜欢这家餐厅，我知道我也会喜欢！”

blocks.html_en: But most of us don’t want to become influencers. If anything, we’ve generally learned the lesson that it sucks to be followed by a bunch of people online. I want to keep my stuff private. I don’t want my opinions to matter to anyone else.

sentences.en: But most of us don’t want to become influencers.
sentences.zh: 但我们大多数人并不想成为有影响力的人。

sentences.en: If anything, we’ve generally learned the lesson that it sucks to be followed by a bunch of people online.
sentences.zh: 如果说有什么不同的话，那就是我们普遍得到的教训是，在网上被一群人关注是很糟糕的。

sentences.en: I want to keep my stuff private.
sentences.zh: 我想保守我的东西的私密性。

sentences.en: I don’t want my opinions to matter to anyone else.
sentences.zh: 我不希望我的意见对其他人来说很重要。

blocks.html_en: I mean sure, when someone comes to town I want to be able to give them a list of restaurants they should visit and activities they should do. But I want to send it to them, not the rest of the world.

sentences.en: I mean sure, when someone comes to town I want to be able to give them a list of restaurants they should visit and activities they should do.
sentences.zh: 我的意思是，当然，当有人来到城里时，我希望能够给他们一份他们应该去的餐馆和他们应该做的活动的清单。

sentences.en: But I want to send it to them, not the rest of the world.
sentences.zh: 但我想把它发送给他们，而不是世界其他地方。

blocks.html_en: We can split the world into three kinds of people:

sentences.en: We can split the world into three kinds of people:
sentences.zh: 我们可以把世界分为三种人：

blocks.html_en: • Those who would only record a review if other people will read it. These are the influencer-wannabes

sentences.en: Those who would only record a review if other people will read it.
sentences.zh: 那些只有在其他人阅读时才会记录评论的人。

sentences.en: These are the influencer-wannabes
sentences.zh: 这些都是有影响力的人

blocks.html_en: • People like me, who would provide a mix of public and private reviews, depending on the circumstance

sentences.en: People like me, who would provide a mix of public and private reviews, depending on the circumstance
sentences.zh: 像我这样的人，会根据具体情况提供公开和私人的评论

blocks.html_en: • People like my wife who will only ever record reviews if they can be confident it will be private to just their friend group, or even just to themselves

sentences.en: People like my wife who will only ever record reviews if they can be confident it will be private to just their friend group, or even just to themselves
sentences.zh: 像我妻子这样的人，只有在确信评论仅对他们的朋友群甚至是他们自己保密时才会记录评论

blocks.html_en: The current crop of applications works fine for the first group. But it leaves the latter two out in the cold.

sentences.en: The current crop of applications works fine for the first group.
sentences.zh: 当前的一批应用程序对于第一组来说效果很好。

sentences.en: But it leaves the latter two out in the cold.
sentences.zh: 但它却把后两者排除在外。

blocks.html_en: And I bet anything that those other two groups are much larger.

sentences.en: And I bet anything that those other two groups are much larger.
sentences.zh: 我敢打赌，其他两组要大得多。

blocks.html_en: So, what we need is a system that allows application developers to easily let their users choose how public or private they want to be – entirely public, entirely private, or sharing piecemeal with individual groups.

sentences.en: So, what we need is a system that allows application developers to easily let their users choose how public or private they want to be – entirely public, entirely private, or sharing piecemeal with individual groups.
sentences.zh: 因此，我们需要的是一个系统，允许应用程序开发人员轻松地让用户选择他们想要的公开或私有程度 - 完全公开、完全私有或与各个组共享。

blocks.html_en: What does this have to do with ATProto?

sentences.en: What does this have to do with ATProto?
sentences.zh: 这和 ATProto 有什么关系？

blocks.html_en: Let’s start with what’s great about ATProto: It seems to be the first protocol designed to solve identity at scale, enabling any application to build ATProto identities into their application for authentication, tracking followers and following, and the other related features that basically every application needs.

sentences.en: Let’s start with what’s great about ATProto: It seems to be the first protocol designed to solve identity at scale, enabling any application to build ATProto identities into their application for authentication, tracking followers and following, and the other related features that basically every application needs.
sentences.zh: 让我们从 ATProto 的优点开始：它似乎是第一个旨在大规模解决身份问题的协议，使任何应用程序都可以将 ATProto 身份构建到其应用程序中，以进行身份​​验证、跟踪关注者和关注，以及基本上每个应用程序都需要的其他相关功能。

blocks.html_en: Its identity system not perfect (I wish it were more human readable, especially). But it seems to be close enough today that nearly every speaker at Local First this year mentioned it. No more does every app developer have to create their own social graph, their own identity and authentication systems, their own means of finding friends.

sentences.en: Its identity system not perfect (I wish it were more human readable, especially).
sentences.zh: 它的身份系统并不完美（我希望它更具人类可读性，尤其是）。

sentences.en: But it seems to be close enough today that nearly every speaker at Local First this year mentioned it.
sentences.zh: 但今天似乎已经足够接近了，今年 Local First 的几乎每个演讲者都提到了它。

sentences.en: No more does every app developer have to create their own social graph, their own identity and authentication systems, their own means of finding friends.
sentences.zh: 每个应用程序开发人员不再需要创建自己的社交图谱、自己的身份和身份验证系统以及自己的寻找朋友的方式。

blocks.html_en: Unfortunately, at least for now, the rest of the protocol is of limited use for my goals.

sentences.en: Unfortunately, at least for now, the rest of the protocol is of limited use for my goals.
sentences.zh: 不幸的是，至少目前，该协议的其余部分对我的目标的用途有限。

blocks.html_en: Today, ATProto is public-only: It assumes that everything you do will be published online for the whole world to see. That design decision is baked into everything from the storage systems to the structure of the publishing services.

sentences.en: Today, ATProto is public-only: It assumes that everything you do will be published online for the whole world to see.
sentences.zh: 如今，ATProto 仅限公开：它假设您所做的一切都将在线发布，供全世界查看。

sentences.en: That design decision is baked into everything from the storage systems to the structure of the publishing services.
sentences.zh: 该设计决策融入了从存储系统到发布服务结构的各个方面。

blocks.html_en: The community is currently designing what they call “permissioned data”. (I think this is a silly name, and it should just be called “private data”, which is better both because “private” is actually a word, and also because it describes user behavior instead of technical implementation, which is a far better naming practice).

sentences.en: The community is currently designing what they call “permissioned data”.
sentences.zh: 社区目前正在设计他们所谓的“许可数据”。

sentences.en: (I think this is a silly name, and it should just be called “private data”, which is better both because “private” is actually a word, and also because it describes user behavior instead of technical implementation, which is a far better naming practice).
sentences.zh: （我认为这是一个愚蠢的名字，它应该被称为“私有数据”，这更好，因为“私有”实际上是一个词，而且因为它描述了用户行为而不是技术实现，这是一个更好的命名实践）。

blocks.html_en: Unfortunately, while most of the assumptions driving the design seem reasonable, the resulting design looks very hard to build on. A lot of where I think it goes wrong is captured perfectly in the above linked post:

sentences.en: Unfortunately, while most of the assumptions driving the design seem reasonable, the resulting design looks very hard to build on.
sentences.zh: 不幸的是，虽然驱动设计的大多数假设似乎都是合理的，但最终的设计看起来很难建立。

sentences.en: A lot of where I think it goes wrong is captured perfectly in the above linked post:
sentences.zh: 我认为出现问题的很多地方都在上面的链接帖子中完美地捕捉到了：

blocks.html_en: Before we get into it, the through-line on all of this is that public broadcast data is substantially different from permissioned data.

sentences.en: Before we get into it, the through-line on all of this is that public broadcast data is substantially different from permissioned data.
sentences.zh: 在我们开始讨论之前，所有这一切的要点是公共广播数据与许可数据有很大不同。

blocks.html_en: Before we get into it, the through-line on all of this is that public broadcast data is substantially different from permissioned data.

sentences.en: Before we get into it, the through-line on all of this is that public broadcast data is substantially different from permissioned data.
sentences.zh: 在我们开始讨论之前，所有这一切的要点是公共广播数据与许可数据有很大不同。

blocks.html_en: I fundamentally disagree with this. (I am not the only one.)

sentences.en: I fundamentally disagree with this.
sentences.zh: 我从根本上不同意这一点。

sentences.en: (I am not the only one.)
sentences.zh: （我不是唯一一个。）

blocks.html_en: Private and public data are basically identical. A restaurant review is a restaurant review whether I am the only one who ever sees it or it gets shouted to the rooftops. A book review I share with my book club is identical to a book review I share with my wife or publish on my web site.

sentences.en: Private and public data are basically identical.
sentences.zh: 私人和公共数据基本相同。

sentences.en: A restaurant review is a restaurant review whether I am the only one who ever sees it or it gets shouted to the rooftops.
sentences.zh: 餐厅评论就是餐厅评论，无论我是唯一看到它的人还是它被大声喊到屋顶。

sentences.en: A book review I share with my book club is identical to a book review I share with my wife or publish on my web site.
sentences.zh: 我与读书俱乐部分享的书评与我与妻子分享或在我的网站上发布的书评相同。

blocks.html_en: Data I publish to the world is just a special case: The permission is world-read. Just like data I never publish is a special case: It has no readers other than me.

sentences.en: Data I publish to the world is just a special case: The permission is world-read.
sentences.zh: 我向全世界发布的数据只是一个特例：权限是全世界可读的。

sentences.en: Just like data I never publish is a special case: It has no readers other than me.
sentences.zh: 就像我从未发布的数据是一个特例：除了我之外，它没有读者。

blocks.html_en: Unfortunately ATProto is already used out in the wild for Bluesky, and it is designed and built as a public-only protocol. It would be hard to change it to support access control, limited distribution, and the other features you need with private data.

sentences.en: Unfortunately ATProto is already used out in the wild for Bluesky, and it is designed and built as a public-only protocol.
sentences.zh: 不幸的是 ATProto 已经在 Bluesky 中广泛使用，并且它被设计和构建为仅供公共使用的协议。

sentences.en: It would be hard to change it to support access control, limited distribution, and the other features you need with private data.
sentences.zh: 很难对其进行更改以支持访问控制、有限分发以及私有数据所需的其他功能。

blocks.html_en: So, instead, the community has, from what I can tell, just… designed a completely independent system for private data.

sentences.en: So, instead, the community has, from what I can tell, just…
sentences.zh: 因此，据我所知，社区只是……

sentences.en: designed a completely independent system for private data.
sentences.zh: 为私有数据设计了一个完全独立的系统。

blocks.html_en: Permissioned Data relies on the existing identity system and lexicons (for defining data types). But adds entirely new data structures, plus new methods for managing and validating that data. (Note that the link above is to one in a series of posts on the design; you can get to all of them from there.)

sentences.en: Permissioned Data relies on the existing identity system and lexicons (for defining data types).
sentences.zh: 许可数据依赖于现有的身份系统和词典（用于定义数据类型）。

sentences.en: But adds entirely new data structures, plus new methods for managing and validating that data.
sentences.zh: 但添加了全新的数据结构，以及管理和验证该数据的新方法。

sentences.en: (Note that the link above is to one in a series of posts on the design; you can get to all of them from there.)
sentences.zh: （请注意，上面的链接是有关该设计的一系列帖子中的一个；您可以从那里访问所有帖子。）

blocks.html_en: So, as an app developer, you have to essentially write two applications: One for public data, and one for private. But, your users don’t think of it as two apps. “These are restaurant reviews. All the restaurant reviews should go together.” So your job, as the developer, is to support these two data systems, and two protocols, but never let the user see that they’re totally different.

sentences.en: So, as an app developer, you have to essentially write two applications: One for public data, and one for private.
sentences.zh: 因此，作为一名应用程序开发人员，您基本上必须编写两个应用程序：一个用于公共数据，另一个用于私有数据。

sentences.en: But, your users don’t think of it as two apps.
sentences.zh: 但是，您的用户并不认为它是两个应用程序。

sentences.en: “These are restaurant reviews.
sentences.zh: “这些是餐厅评论。

sentences.en: All the restaurant reviews should go together.”
sentences.zh: 所有的餐厅评论都应该放在一起。”

sentences.en: So your job, as the developer, is to support these two data systems, and two protocols, but never let the user see that they’re totally different.
sentences.zh: 因此，作为开发人员，你的工作就是支持这两个数据系统和两个协议，但永远不要让用户看到它们完全不同。

blocks.html_en: That gets messy real fast. You’ve got a private post you want to make public? You’re not modifying it – you’re deleting the old one and making a new one in the public subsystem. Does it keep its likes? Its reposts? Links to it? 🤷‍♂️ No idea, but… probably not.

sentences.en: That gets messy real fast.
sentences.zh: 这很快就会变得混乱。

sentences.en: You’ve got a private post you want to make public?
sentences.zh: 您有想要公开的私人帖子吗？

sentences.en: You’re not modifying it – you’re deleting the old one and making a new one in the public subsystem.
sentences.zh: 你不是在修改它——而是删除旧的并在公共子系统中创建一个新的。

sentences.en: Does it keep its likes?
sentences.zh: 它会保留自己的喜好吗？

sentences.en: Its reposts?
sentences.zh: 它的转发？

sentences.en: Links to it? 🤷‍♂️ No idea, but…
sentences.zh: 有链接吗？ 🤷‍♂️ 不知道，但是……

sentences.en: probably not.
sentences.zh: 可能不会。

blocks.html_en: It’s actually worse than that. ATProto’s storage subsystem, the “Personal Data Server”, provides direct access to your data. In other words, you should expect more than one application to want to read and write your data. So now, anyone who is interested in this data has to write two versions, but lie to their users and hide any differences.

sentences.en: It’s actually worse than that.
sentences.zh: 实际上比这更糟糕。

sentences.en: ATProto’s storage subsystem, the “Personal Data Server”, provides direct access to your data.
sentences.zh: ATProto 的存储子系统“个人数据服务器”提供对数据的直接访问。

sentences.en: In other words, you should expect more than one application to want to read and write your data.
sentences.zh: 换句话说，您应该预期有多个应用程序想要读取和写入您的数据。

sentences.en: So now, anyone who is interested in this data has to write two versions, but lie to their users and hide any differences.
sentences.zh: 所以现在，任何对此数据感兴趣的人都必须编写两个版本，但对用户撒谎并隐藏任何差异。

blocks.html_en: Because, again, there aren’t actual differences. The actual data being stored is the same. The way the user thinks about it is the same.

sentences.en: Because, again, there aren’t actual differences.
sentences.zh: 因为，同样，不存在实际差异。

sentences.en: The actual data being stored is the same.
sentences.zh: 实际存储的数据是相同的。

sentences.en: The way the user thinks about it is the same.
sentences.zh: 用户的思考方式是一样的。

blocks.html_en: To me this is a sign that the current proposal is flawed.

sentences.en: To me this is a sign that the current proposal is flawed.
sentences.zh: 对我来说，这表明当前的提案存在缺陷。

blocks.html_en: I don’t know how to fix it. I’m nowhere near close enough to conversation to think I can propose solutions yet. But hopefully I can help people see problems, at least.

sentences.en: I don’t know how to fix it.
sentences.zh: 我不知道如何解决它。

sentences.en: I’m nowhere near close enough to conversation to think I can propose solutions yet.
sentences.zh: 我距离对话还差得远，还不足以提出解决方案。

sentences.en: But hopefully I can help people see problems, at least.
sentences.zh: 但希望我至少能帮助人们看到问题。

blocks.html_en: One last local-first note

sentences.en: One last local-first note
sentences.zh: 最后一个本地第一音符

blocks.html_en: I only had a high-level understanding of ATProto went I went to Local First, so I learned a lot while I was there. One thing I was definitely wrong about was how the PDS worked.

sentences.en: I only had a high-level understanding of ATProto went I went to Local First, so I learned a lot while I was there.
sentences.zh: 我去了 Local First 后才对 ATProto 有了较高的了解，所以我在那里学到了很多东西。

sentences.en: One thing I was definitely wrong about was how the PDS worked.
sentences.zh: 我绝对错误的一件事是 PDS 的工作原理。

blocks.html_en: I naively assumed it worked a lot like a git repository. The ATProto community regularly says that I own my own data. I tend to assume that means that I have a copy of it, and that when I make changes, I am operating against my copy then distributing it. This is exactly how git works: I clone a repository, make my changes, then push them back up to the server for distribution. I have a hard time imagining “owning” my data without always having a copy of it.

sentences.en: I naively assumed it worked a lot like a git repository.
sentences.zh: 我天真地认为它的工作原理很像 git 存储库。

sentences.en: The ATProto community regularly says that I own my own data.
sentences.zh: ATProto 社区经常说我拥有自己的数据。

sentences.en: I tend to assume that means that I have a copy of it, and that when I make changes, I am operating against my copy then distributing it.
sentences.zh: 我倾向于认为这意味着我有它的副本，并且当我进行更改时，我是针对我的副本进行操作然后分发它。

sentences.en: This is exactly how git works: I clone a repository, make my changes, then push them back up to the server for distribution.
sentences.zh: 这正是 git 的工作原理：我克隆一个存储库，进行更改，然后将它们推送回服务器进行分发。

sentences.en: I have a hard time imagining “owning” my data without always having a copy of it.
sentences.zh: 我很难想象“拥有”我的数据而不总是拥有它的副本。

blocks.html_en: But no, that’s not at all how ATProto works. The PDS is “your” server, but… it’s actually a server. You do not push data up and pull it down; you speak a protocol to it. You only “own” your data because it and the protocols for accessing it are public, and you can change where it is stored.

sentences.en: But no, that’s not at all how ATProto works.
sentences.zh: 但不，这根本不是 ATProto 的工作原理。

sentences.en: The PDS is “your” server, but…
sentences.zh: PDS 是“您的”服务器，但是……

sentences.en: it’s actually a server.
sentences.zh: 它实际上是一个服务器。

sentences.en: You do not push data up and pull it down; you speak a protocol to it.
sentences.zh: 您不会将数据上推或下拉；而是将其上推或拉下。你对它讲一个协议。

sentences.en: You only “own” your data because it and the protocols for accessing it are public, and you can change where it is stored.
sentences.zh: 您仅“拥有”您的数据，因为它和访问它的协议是公开的，并且您可以更改它的存储位置。

blocks.html_en: It does use cryptography-based data integrity guarantees like git does, which is one more reason I assumed I could easily manage the data like git does. But those guarantees are only used on the server, not in the protocols you use to provide new data.

sentences.en: It does use cryptography-based data integrity guarantees like git does, which is one more reason I assumed I could easily manage the data like git does.
sentences.zh: 它确实像 git 一样使用基于加密的数据完整性保证，这也是我认为我可以像 git 一样轻松管理数据的另一个原因。

sentences.en: But those guarantees are only used on the server, not in the protocols you use to provide new data.
sentences.zh: 但这些保证仅在服务器上使用，而不在您用于提供新数据的协议中使用。

blocks.html_en: ATProto meets about half of the design principles in the Local First essay linked above. But it clearly misses on the data being at your fingertips or the network being optional, and also misses out on a lot of implied abilities because you’re always talking to a remote server.

sentences.en: ATProto meets about half of the design principles in the Local First essay linked above.
sentences.zh: ATProto 满足上面链接的本地优先论文中大约一半的设计原则。

sentences.en: But it clearly misses on the data being at your fingertips or the network being optional, and also misses out on a lot of implied abilities because you’re always talking to a remote server.
sentences.zh: 但它显然错过了触手可及的数据或可选的网络，并且还错过了许多隐含的功能，因为你总是与远程服务器通信。

blocks.html_en: If you want to record a review while you’re offline… best practice is to essentially store those reviews in a temporary area, then push them to the server when you’re back online. In other words, you have to build your own custom storage and sync system if you want offline support. Obviously not impossible, but also obviously not local-first.

sentences.en: If you want to record a review while you’re offline…
sentences.zh: 如果您想在离线时记录评论...

sentences.en: best practice is to essentially store those reviews in a temporary area, then push them to the server when you’re back online.
sentences.zh: 最佳实践是将这些评论存储在临时区域中，然后在您重新上线时将它们推送到服务器。

sentences.en: In other words, you have to build your own custom storage and sync system if you want offline support.
sentences.zh: 换句话说，如果您需要离线支持，则必须构建自己的自定义存储和同步系统。

sentences.en: Obviously not impossible, but also obviously not local-first.
sentences.zh: 显然并非不可能，但也显然不是本地优先。

blocks.html_en: If you then cross this with the current design for Permissioned Data, I’ve got a bit of a mess:

sentences.en: If you then cross this with the current design for Permissioned Data, I’ve got a bit of a mess:
sentences.zh: 如果你将其与当前的许可数据设计相比较，我会遇到一些混乱：

blocks.html_en: • Custom local storage for offline data

sentences.en: Custom local storage for offline data
sentences.zh: 离线数据的自定义本地存储

blocks.html_en: • Custom sync system for emptying offline data

sentences.en: Custom sync system for emptying offline data
sentences.zh: 用于清空离线数据的自定义同步系统

blocks.html_en: • Sync system must use different protocols for public and private data

sentences.en: Sync system must use different protocols for public and private data
sentences.zh: 同步系统必须对公共和私有数据使用不同的协议

blocks.html_en: • Online app usage must also use separate read and write systems for private and public data, and is likely different from the system used for offline support

sentences.en: Online app usage must also use separate read and write systems for private and public data, and is likely different from the system used for offline support
sentences.zh: 在线应用程序的使用还必须使用单独的读写系统来存储私有和公共数据，并且可能与用于离线支持的系统不同

blocks.html_en: As an application developer committed to both local first principles and letting the user choose between public and private, ATProto is working at least as much against me as with me. Am I really better off building on this, versus designing my own system? That question is doubly scary when you recognize that the protocol designer and I are so far apart philosophically.

sentences.en: As an application developer committed to both local first principles and letting the user choose between public and private, ATProto is working at least as much against me as with me.
sentences.zh: 作为一名致力于本地第一原则并让用户在公共和私有之间进行选择的应用程序开发人员，ATProto 对我的影响至少与对我的影响一样大。

sentences.en: Am I really better off building on this, versus designing my own system?
sentences.zh: 与设计自己的系统相比，在此基础上构建真的更好吗？

sentences.en: That question is doubly scary when you recognize that the protocol designer and I are so far apart philosophically.
sentences.zh: 当你认识到协议设计者和我在哲学上相距甚远时，这个问题就加倍可怕了。

blocks.html_en: If I believe public and private data are fundamentally the same, just with different access rights, but the community believes they are and should be different… I am fighting the protocol, storage system, and community every step of the way. I’ve done this before, and it sucks.

sentences.en: If I believe public and private data are fundamentally the same, just with different access rights, but the community believes they are and should be different…
sentences.zh: 如果我相信公共数据和私人数据本质上是相同的，只是访问权限不同，但社区认为它们是并且应该是不同的......

sentences.en: I am fighting the protocol, storage system, and community every step of the way.
sentences.zh: 我每一步都在与协议、存储系统和社区作斗争。

sentences.en: I’ve done this before, and it sucks.
sentences.zh: 我以前做过这个，而且很糟糕。

blocks.html_en: Conclusion

sentences.en: Conclusion
sentences.zh: 结论

blocks.html_en: I’m still excited about ATProto. And thankfully, the Permissioned Data design is early enough that it can still be improved. And I’m not the only person out there pushing back on the existing design and its goals.

sentences.en: I’m still excited about ATProto.
sentences.zh: 我仍然对 ATProto 感到兴奋。

sentences.en: And thankfully, the Permissioned Data design is early enough that it can still be improved.
sentences.zh: 值得庆幸的是，许可数据的设计足够早，仍然可以改进。

sentences.en: And I’m not the only person out there pushing back on the existing design and its goals.
sentences.zh: 我并不是唯一一个反对现有设计及其目标的人。

blocks.html_en: Even if the proposed design goes through, I can build at least somewhat on ATProto, maybe using its identity and lexicon systems.

sentences.en: Even if the proposed design goes through, I can build at least somewhat on ATProto, maybe using its identity and lexicon systems.
sentences.zh: 即使提议的设计得以通过，我至少可以在 ATProto 上进行一些构建，也许可以使用它的身份和词典系统。

blocks.html_en: But I came up in the 90s, when the entire internet was built on standardized protocols that were internet-scale, resilient, maintained by the community and a standards group, and built to empower its users rather than enrich whoever could build the biggest moat. I want that world again.

sentences.en: But I came up in the 90s, when the entire internet was built on standardized protocols that were internet-scale, resilient, maintained by the community and a standards group, and built to empower its users rather than enrich whoever could build the biggest moat.
sentences.zh: 但我是在 90 年代出现的，当时整个互联网都是建立在标准化协议之上的，这些协议具有互联网规模、有弹性，由社区和标准组织维护，旨在增强用户能力，而不是让那些能够建造最大护城河的人变得富有。

sentences.en: I want that world again.
sentences.zh: 我想要再次那个世界。

blocks.html_en: I think ATProto has the opportunity to be the first new protocol of this type in decades. I hope they go for it, and build something every application developer (but especially me!) wants to build on.

sentences.en: I think ATProto has the opportunity to be the first new protocol of this type in decades.
sentences.zh: 我认为 ATProto 有机会成为几十年来第一个此类新协议。

sentences.en: I hope they go for it, and build something every application developer (but especially me!)
sentences.zh: 我希望他们能够继续努力，为每个应用程序开发人员（尤其是我！）构建一些东西。

sentences.en: wants to build on.
sentences.zh: 想要继续发展。

content_html_zh:
基于 ATProto 构建

发布日期：2026 年 7 月 20 日

Bluesky 的 Atmosphere 协议有机会成为全代应用程序的基础。 但它正在以令人惊讶和令人失望的方式发展。

上周我花了一部分时间参加在柏林举行的本地优先会议。 当我发现每个人都在关注人工智能编码时，我并不感到惊讶。 不太出乎意料的是 ATProto 的普及程度。

几个月来我一直在考虑尝试在 ATProto 上进行构建，所以这是一个很好的机会，可以向构建它的人和实际构建它的人（多个关键的 Bluesky 人员都在场）提出一系列问题。

我希望 ATProto 成为我的答案。 我想再次生活在一个应用程序开发人员使用公共、社区驱动的标准来创建具有通用数据标准的可互操作应用程序的世界。 我认为可以。 但不幸的是，我认为目前还没有实现这一目标。

在这篇文章中，我将描述我一直想要构建的内容，以及 ATProto 当前和提议的设计如何与此配合使用。 我还将针对令我惊讶的事情发表一些评论。

我可能在这里尝试做太多事情。 但我的主要目标是提供有关拟议设计的反馈，如果不清楚我想要构建什么以及为什么，就很难做到这一点。

从评论开始

我想要构建的最小版本是一套用于记录评论的应用程序。 在理想的情况下，这些应用程序将取代 Yelp、GoodReads、Letterboxd 和一堆类似的应用程序，几乎可以满足您可能考虑查看的任何内容。 （是的，我知道已经有人在 ATProto 上构建这些版本；我想与他们合作。）

我想取代这些应用程序不是因为它们的功能，而是因为它们的商业模式。 我和我的妻子都不会按照它们的预期方式使用它们，我认为大多数人都像我们中的一个或另一个一样。

本地优先数据

对我来说，我不希望这些公司拥有我的数据。 我们在 Yelp 中有一千多个书签，但是…… 他们被困在 Yelp 中。 我不能用它们做任何其他有用的事情：我不能发布它们，我不能共享它们，我不能在它们上编写脚本，我不能对它们进行版本控制。 它们属于 Yelp，而不是我。 而且，坦率地说，他们提供的管理书签列表的体验有点糟糕。

这是一个经典的本地优先用例。 我可以采用一种非常适合撰写评论的工具，另一种使用相同数据但更擅长为我的朋友发布最佳书籍列表的工具，以及另一种可以轻松在我的网站上共享“最佳商业书籍”列表的工具。 没有任何一个应用程序能够完成所有这些工作，但是共享的公共数据模型可以让每个人都拥有他们想要的东西。

我已经不再愿意给予大公司永久的权利来控制我如何使用自己的数据。 （这就是为什么我以纯文本形式写这篇文章并通过 Hugo 发布。） 随着时间的推移，这些应用程序只会变得更糟。 如果我要投入那么多时间来记录我的意见，我想控制它的使用，而不是让 Yelp 这么做。

公共和私人

至于我的妻子，她永远不会在网上发布任何内容。 绝不。 如果我想让她评论书籍、餐馆或食谱，它必须放在一个我——也许还有我们家里的其他人——能看到但其他人看不到的地方。

就我个人而言，我愿意发表公众评论。 有时我特别想分享我认为作为创始人有用的书籍。 但这样做很复杂。 我为自己记录的有关餐厅的信息与我希望更广泛的世界了解的信息截然不同。 然而… 大多数应用程序不允许您进行这种区分。 评论是公开的。 你的观点会影响对象的声誉，即使你不希望如此。 （我是素食主义者并且患有自闭症，所以为我工作的地方不一定与其他人相关。）

从根本上来说，这些应用程序中的大多数都是为了支持影响者而构建的：那些想要在网上建立追随者的评论的人。 “天哪，她推荐了最好的书！” “如果他喜欢这家餐厅，我知道我也会喜欢！”

但我们大多数人并不想成为有影响力的人。 如果说有什么不同的话，那就是我们普遍得到的教训是，在网上被一群人关注是很糟糕的。 我想保守我的东西的私密性。 我不希望我的意见对其他人来说很重要。

我的意思是，当然，当有人来到城里时，我希望能够给他们一份他们应该去的餐馆和他们应该做的活动的清单。 但我想把它发送给他们，而不是世界其他地方。

我们可以把世界分为三种人：

  • 那些只有在其他人阅读时才会记录评论的人。 这些都是有影响力的人
  • 像我这样的人，会根据具体情况提供公开和私人的评论
  • 像我妻子这样的人，只有在确信评论仅对他们的朋友群甚至是他们自己保密时才会记录评论

当前的一批应用程序对于第一组来说效果很好。 但它却把后两者排除在外。

我敢打赌，其他两组要大得多。

因此，我们需要的是一个系统，允许应用程序开发人员轻松地让用户选择他们想要的公开或私有程度 - 完全公开、完全私有或与各个组共享。

这和 ATProto 有什么关系？

让我们从 ATProto 的优点开始：它似乎是第一个旨在大规模解决身份问题的协议，使任何应用程序都可以将 ATProto 身份构建到其应用程序中，以进行身份​​验证、跟踪关注者和关注，以及基本上每个应用程序都需要的其他相关功能。

它的身份系统并不完美（我希望它更具人类可读性，尤其是）。 但今天似乎已经足够接近了，今年 Local First 的几乎每个演讲者都提到了它。 每个应用程序开发人员不再需要创建自己的社交图谱、自己的身份和身份验证系统以及自己的寻找朋友的方式。

不幸的是，至少目前，该协议的其余部分对我的目标的用途有限。

如今，ATProto 仅限公开：它假设您所做的一切都将在线发布，供全世界查看。 该设计决策融入了从存储系统到发布服务结构的各个方面。

社区目前正在设计他们所谓的“许可数据”。 （我认为这是一个愚蠢的名字，它应该被称为“私有数据”，这更好，因为“私有”实际上是一个词，而且因为它描述了用户行为而不是技术实现，这是一个更好的命名实践）。

不幸的是，虽然驱动设计的大多数假设似乎都是合理的，但最终的设计看起来很难建立。 我认为出现问题的很多地方都在上面的链接帖子中完美地捕捉到了：

在我们开始讨论之前，所有这一切的要点是公共广播数据与许可数据有很大不同。

我从根本上不同意这一点。 （我不是唯一一个。）

私人和公共数据基本相同。 餐厅评论就是餐厅评论，无论我是唯一看到它的人还是它被大声喊到屋顶。 我与读书俱乐部分享的书评与我与妻子分享或在我的网站上发布的书评相同。

我向全世界发布的数据只是一个特例：权限是全世界可读的。 就像我从未发布的数据是一个特例：除了我之外，它没有读者。

不幸的是 ATProto 已经在 Bluesky 中广泛使用，并且它被设计和构建为仅供公共使用的协议。 很难对其进行更改以支持访问控制、有限分发以及私有数据所需的其他功能。

因此，据我所知，社区只是…… 为私有数据设计了一个完全独立的系统。

许可数据依赖于现有的身份系统和词典（用于定义数据类型）。 但添加了全新的数据结构，以及管理和验证该数据的新方法。 （请注意，上面的链接是有关该设计的一系列帖子中的一个；您可以从那里访问所有帖子。）

因此，作为一名应用程序开发人员，您基本上必须编写两个应用程序：一个用于公共数据，另一个用于私有数据。 但是，您的用户并不认为它是两个应用程序。 “这些是餐厅评论。 所有的餐厅评论都应该放在一起。” 因此，作为开发人员，你的工作就是支持这两个数据系统和两个协议，但永远不要让用户看到它们完全不同。

这很快就会变得混乱。 您有想要公开的私人帖子吗？ 你不是在修改它——而是删除旧的并在公共子系统中创建一个新的。 它会保留自己的喜好吗？ 它的转发？ 有链接吗？ 🤷‍♂️ 不知道，但是…… 可能不会。

实际上比这更糟糕。 ATProto 的存储子系统“个人数据服务器”提供对数据的直接访问。 换句话说，您应该预期有多个应用程序想要读取和写入您的数据。 所以现在，任何对此数据感兴趣的人都必须编写两个版本，但对用户撒谎并隐藏任何差异。

因为，同样，不存在实际差异。 实际存储的数据是相同的。 用户的思考方式是一样的。

对我来说，这表明当前的提案存在缺陷。

我不知道如何解决它。 我距离对话还差得远，还不足以提出解决方案。 但希望我至少能帮助人们看到问题。

最后一个本地第一音符

我去了 Local First 后才对 ATProto 有了较高的了解，所以我在那里学到了很多东西。 我绝对错误的一件事是 PDS 的工作原理。

我天真地认为它的工作原理很像 git 存储库。 ATProto 社区经常说我拥有自己的数据。 我倾向于认为这意味着我有它的副本，并且当我进行更改时，我是针对我的副本进行操作然后分发它。 这正是 git 的工作原理：我克隆一个存储库，进行更改，然后将它们推送回服务器进行分发。 我很难想象“拥有”我的数据而不总是拥有它的副本。

但不，这根本不是 ATProto 的工作原理。 PDS 是“您的”服务器，但是…… 它实际上是一个服务器。 您不会将数据上推或下拉；而是将其上推或拉下。你对它讲一个协议。 您仅“拥有”您的数据，因为它和访问它的协议是公开的，并且您可以更改它的存储位置。

它确实像 git 一样使用基于加密的数据完整性保证，这也是我认为我可以像 git 一样轻松管理数据的另一个原因。 但这些保证仅在服务器上使用，而不在您用于提供新数据的协议中使用。

ATProto 满足上面链接的本地优先论文中大约一半的设计原则。 但它显然错过了触手可及的数据或可选的网络，并且还错过了许多隐含的功能，因为你总是与远程服务器通信。

如果您想在离线时记录评论... 最佳实践是将这些评论存储在临时区域中，然后在您重新上线时将它们推送到服务器。 换句话说，如果您需要离线支持，则必须构建自己的自定义存储和同步系统。 显然并非不可能，但也显然不是本地优先。

如果你将其与当前的许可数据设计相比较，我会遇到一些混乱：

  • 离线数据的自定义本地存储
  • 用于清空离线数据的自定义同步系统
  • 同步系统必须对公共和私有数据使用不同的协议
  • 在线应用程序的使用还必须使用单独的读写系统来存储私有和公共数据，并且可能与用于离线支持的系统不同

作为一名致力于本地第一原则并让用户在公共和私有之间进行选择的应用程序开发人员，ATProto 对我的影响至少与对我的影响一样大。 与设计自己的系统相比，在此基础上构建真的更好吗？ 当你认识到协议设计者和我在哲学上相距甚远时，这个问题就加倍可怕了。

如果我相信公共数据和私人数据本质上是相同的，只是访问权限不同，但社区认为它们是并且应该是不同的...... 我每一步都在与协议、存储系统和社区作斗争。 我以前做过这个，而且很糟糕。

结论

我仍然对 ATProto 感到兴奋。 值得庆幸的是，许可数据的设计足够早，仍然可以改进。 我并不是唯一一个反对现有设计及其目标的人。

即使提议的设计得以通过，我至少可以在 ATProto 上进行一些构建，也许可以使用它的身份和词典系统。

但我是在 90 年代出现的，当时整个互联网都是建立在标准化协议之上的，这些协议具有互联网规模、有弹性，由社区和标准组织维护，旨在增强用户能力，而不是让那些能够建造最大护城河的人变得富有。 我想要再次那个世界。

我认为 ATProto 有机会成为几十年来第一个此类新协议。 我希望他们能够继续努力，为每个应用程序开发人员（尤其是我！）构建一些东西。 想要继续发展。
